title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Invariant Recall
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa algorithm evidence portfolio."""

from collections import deque
from dataclasses import dataclass

from typing import TypeVar

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Problem Specification correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Problem Specification assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Solve a bounded review instance with 9 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Problem Specification has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement a clear correct baseline before optimizing and preserve it as an oracle.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
